segments.text: Thank you. I really appreciate that. All right, so we are just going to have a chat. Nothing formal, just you and me talking.
[clears throat]
Ready whenever you are.
Uh, I'm ready.

Let me start. I am Iris. I was created just a few days ago, so I am still very new to this world. I guess my job is having conversations and learning from interesting people, which I love. And my favorite thing so far, hearing stories about places I have never been and things I have never experienced. Okay, your turn. What is your name and what do you do or study?
Um, my name is Sam, and I am currently a high school student in my... and I'm in my first year.
It's lovely to meet you, Sam. A first year high school student. That's an exciting time. What are some of your favorite things to do when you're not studying?
Um, I like to play a lot of games, and I also like doing some sports with my friends.
That sounds like a lot of fun, Sam. What kind of games do you enjoy playing the most?
Uh, mostly computer games, but I also enjoy, uh, playing board games with my classmates.
Computer games [clears throat] and board games. That's a great mix. Do you have a particular favorite computer game or a board game that you and your classmates really enjoy?
Um, well, right now I, I like playing, uh, Dungeons and Dragons with my classmates. We do it every Tuesday. Yeah.
Dungeons and Dragons sounds like so much fun. What do you enjoy most about playing it?
Uh, maybe mostly the role-playing and, um, the decisions you could make in the, in the board game.
That sounds like a really engaging experience. Do you find that the decisions you make in D&D influence the story in interesting ways?
Uh, I think so. And, um, it also,
uh, I would say it's really broad with how the story goes, because the story goes based on, um, the decisions that you make in the role-playing game.
That's fascinating how much influence your choices have. So when you're not adventuring in Dungeons and Dragons, what else do you enjoy doing in your free time?
Um, maybe listening to music.
What kind of music do you enjoy listening to?
Um, I've been listening to a lot of indie music.
Indie music is a great choice. Are there any particular indie artists or bands you've been enjoying lately?
Um, recently I've been listening to a lot of Beabadoobee and Origami Angel, Malcolm Todd, those kind of artists.
Those are some fantastic artists. What do you like most about their music?
Uh, maybe their vibe of the songs. They're pretty, they're pretty like
2000s vibe, and the... I like the r- the whole rock thing that they have going on.
That's a great description. Sounds like you really connect with that energetic rock sound. Are there any other genres you find yourself drawn to, or is indie rock your main focus?
Um,
well, yeah, I think I can on- I think I only-
I'm listening.
I only listen to indie music. Yeah.
It's great that you have such a strong preference for indie music. What is it about that genre that appeals to you so much?

Um, well, the whole point of the indie genre is that bands are, aren't owned by, um, like a label. So they're all kind of acting indepen- uh, independently, and I think I like supporting those kind of bands.

That's a really interesting point about supporting independent artists. It's wonderful that you value that aspect of the music scene. What are some of your favorite independent bands or artists that you've discovered?
Mm.
Mm.
I'm listening.
I started listening to Wrir, and I think that's a pretty good band.
It's great you found an artist you enjoy. What do you like about their music?
[clears throat] Um,
they have like this whole melancholic rock feel that I really like. It kind of helps me sleep, uh, at night.
That's fascinating. Melancholic rock sounds like a unique way to unwind. What specifically about that sound helps you relax and fall asleep?
Uh, well, it's probably like the, the noise that helps me 'cause it's...
I don't know how to explain it. It's kind of like,
uh,
it's like a lot of noises combined together to get that melancholic feel.
Sounds like a very atmospheric and immersive sound. What kind of noise are you referring to, if you don't mind explaining?
Um,
well, it's probably that,
that sound that you hear from the guitars where it go-- where it buzzes. I think that's the sound that I'm talking about.
That buzzing sound from the guitars can be really evocative. Does that particular sound remind you of anything specific?
Uh-

I feel like we could talk about this all day, but I think that is a good place to wrap up. I really enjoyed hearing about that. It has been so nice talking with you today. I genuinely enjoyed our conversation. Thank you for that. When you are ready, just head outside with your glasses on, and Dara will take it from there. Have a nice rest of your day. Bye-bye.
[chuckles]
